subsystem.function: Main spar, ribs, carry-through, fuselage frames; continuous load path

instruction: Design only this subsystem: P0 plan, part_tree children, 12-24 coordinate-bearing geometry_ops. Every evidence_feature must appear. Do not modify other subsystems or the global envelope.

criteria.good: Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings, spar sockets, or mating pads are visible and labeled.
criteria.bad: Interfaces exist only as abstract neighbor references.
criteria.loop_feedback: Add visible datum pads or axis/rail/socket features and label their mating function.

criteria.good: Critical slip, snap, pin, hinge, and slide interfaces state plausible clearances.
criteria.bad: Moving/removable joints have no clearance or fit language.
criteria.loop_feedback: Add clearance_mm or tolerance notes for hinge pins, slide rails, sockets, and removable cartridges.

criteria.good: Each subsystem has at least three adjacent interfaces or datums.
criteria.bad: Subsystem is isolated and does not show assembly neighbors.
criteria.loop_feedback: Add at least three adjacent interfaces: mount, service, electrical/data, flow, or inspection datum.

criteria.good: Fits are named with standard intent: clearance fit for sliding, transition for locating, interference/press only where justified, with a numeric allowance.
criteria.bad: All mating diameters are modeled line-to-line with identical nominal dimensions and no fit class or allowance.
criteria.loop_feedback: Classify each mating diameter as clearance/transition/press fit and add a numeric allowance instead of line-to-line nominals.

criteria.good: Holes, slots, and pockets match real catalog hardware sizes (M3/M4 clearance holes, standard bearing OD, common pin diameters).
criteria.bad: Hardware features use arbitrary dimensions that match no purchasable fastener, bearing, or pin.
criteria.loop_feedback: Snap hole, pin, and bearing dimensions to standard catalog sizes and name the intended hardware in callouts.

criteria.good: Spar socket, anti-rotation keys, and center datum remain visible and inspectable.
criteria.bad: Wing/fuselage integration hides spar alignment evidence.
criteria.loop_feedback: Expose spar sockets, anti-rotation keys, and center datum labels in geometry and callouts.

criteria.good: Payload bay is labeled non-weapon/academic non-operational with removable mission/sensor module.
criteria.bad: Design drifts into weaponized or operational payload details.
criteria.loop_feedback: Keep payload as non-weapon educational sensor bay and preserve removable insert/service window.

criteria.good: Battery bay uses slide rail, CG marks, and independent payload/battery removal.
criteria.bad: Battery is buried or coupled to payload in a way that blocks CG adjustment.
criteria.loop_feedback: Add visible CG rail marks and independent slide-in battery/payload service paths.

criteria.good: Lift, landing, and payload loads trace through continuous structure: spar to carry-through to fuselage frames, with fittings at every load transfer.
criteria.bad: Structural members end at skins or bays with no fitting, frame, or carry-through; load path breaks at the wing root.
criteria.loop_feedback: Trace each major load to ground through named members and add fittings or frames wherever the path currently breaks.

criteria.good: P0 internal_feature_targets appear as part_tree children and geometry_ops ids or targets.
criteria.bad: P0 lists features but geometry_ops are generic boxes at the origin.

criteria.good: Most geometry_ops include args.at coordinates distributed across the local subsystem volume.
criteria.bad: Geometry is stacked at [0,0,0] or lacks coordinates.

criteria.good: Uses drill, pocket, boss, channel, pattern_polar, fillet, chamfer — not just boxes.
criteria.bad: Only box and cylinder ops with no subtractive or finishing features.

criteria.good: Every part_tree child has 4+ dedicated geometry_ops (body+subtract+interface+finish).
criteria.bad: Some children have 0-1 ops (LOW-RES). All detail concentrated on primary body.

criteria.good: Uses seed-characteristic ops: chamfer, subtract, drill, channel. Subtractive features (drill/pocket/channel) and finishing (fillet/chamfer) both present.
criteria.bad: Only basic box/cylinder ops. Missing subtractive or finishing features typical for this seed.

criteria.good: Dimensions match long_range_recon_wing scale: 800-3000mm wingspan, 100-250mm chord. Wall thickness, hole diameters, fillet radii are physically plausible.
criteria.bad: Dimensions are placeholder (all 10mm) or unrealistic for the subsystem scale.

citable_sources.title: NASA Systems Engineering Handbook SP-2016-6105 Rev2

boundary_note: Subsystem scale: 800-3000mm wingspan, 100-250mm chord. Environment: outdoor flight, -10 to +45C, dust/humidity. Design loads: wing bending 3g, servo 2-8Nm, catapult launch 15g. Structural: include fixture_points with [x,y,z] and type (fixed/pinned) in verify.boundary_conditions.

operability_profile.operating_principle: Fixed-wing UAV architecture that carries lift through spar/rib stations, routes propulsion/power through the fuselage, and isolates payload/sensor bays.
